topic.what: Outsourced cardinality PSI with secret-shared outputs for aggregated pairwise analytics
topic.how: Uses pseudorandom functions for oblivious set encoding and circuit-PSI between two non-colluding servers to compute secret-shared intersection cardinalities
topic.why_matters: Enables privacy-preserving analytics across multiple entities revealing only aggregate statistics while hiding individual pairwise intersections and dataset sizes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Wall-clock time for computing all pairwise PSI operations in parallel
characteristics.context: Cross-device scenario with 15k employees across 100 entities

characteristics.what_it_measures: Preprocessing time for oblivious transfer extensions setup
characteristics.context: Can be performed beforehand in practice

characteristics.what_itasures: Total data exchanged between servers per pairwise execution
characteristics.context: Cross-silo with 3 large entities scales better than cross-device

characteristics.what_it_measures: Per-pair execution time in parallel setting
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 realistic dataset with 100 entities 15k employees

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total communication for realistic dataset scenario
How parallel computation time changes with number of entities
characteristics.evidence: Table 1a shows decreasing parallel time per entity as n grows
characteristics.context: Due to fixed total employees distributed across more entities

characteristics.what_it_measures: Differential privacy parameter for output perturbation
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.3 DP instantiation with geometric mechanism
characteristics.context: Uses sensitivity Δ = max mails per worker per day

good_for: cross-device scenarios with many small entities
large-scale mail analytics across corporate subsidiaries

novelty: First primitive combining outsourced PSI with secret-shared hidden outputs
Aggregated pairwise cardinality PSI allowing sum of all intersections
Privacy-preserving mail analytics with differential privacy
Formal security proofs in semi-honest model with two-server architecture
open_problems: Supporting analytics beyond cardinality (e.g., mail size aggregation)
Hybrid unbalanced/balanced cPSI architecture for heterogeneous entity sizes
